race.comment: CABBUCIO (6) got off the mark over 1400m at Kembla Grange last start, scoring by a neck. Impressed placing at Nowra first-up prior, and the lightly-raced filly has plenty of scope for improvement. Jean Van Overmeire retains the mount, and she's a big chance to go back-to-back. LUNA BAY (7) resumed over a sprint at Newcastle and will derive fitness benefit from that. Has come off Stakes company at Flemington and brought strong NSW provincial mile form to that. She won over 1500m at Kembla Grange at her only second-up outing, and the step up in trip is ideal. CANADIAN FLING (5) led but was safely held to the line over 1500m at Hawkesbury. In a top Sydney barn and can stick on better in this company. TIMELESS GRACE (8) jumped slowly but really knuckled down in the straight when beaten a whisker over 1400m at Wagga. Gives every indication the 1600m will suit, but she does need to jump cleaner. JVO is off, and Boudvillain goes on.
